HR6.reason: American Dream and Promise Act - provides a pathway to legal permanent resident status and eventually citizenship for DACA recipients (Dreamers) and TPS holders

HR1603.reason: Farm Workforce Modernization Act (117th) - creates a pathway to legal status for undocumented agricultural workers, related to but broader than DACA; supports immigrant legalization

HR5038.reason: Farm Workforce Modernization Act (116th) - creates a pathway to legal status for undocumented agricultural workers, supporting immigrant legalization

HR1333.reason: NO BAN Act - prohibits religious-based immigration bans and limits executive authority to restrict immigration, supporting immigrant protections broadly

HR1573.reason: Access to Counsel Act - ensures immigrants in removal proceedings have access to legal counsel, supporting due process protections for immigrants including DACA-eligible individuals

SJRES46.reason: CRA disapproval of Trump-era DOJ/DHS rule restricting asylum procedures - voting Yea blocks rules that made it harder to claim asylum, supporting immigrant protections

HR29.reason: Laken Riley Act (119th) - requires ICE detention of undocumented immigrants charged with certain crimes, part of broader anti-immigrant enforcement framework that opposes protections like DACA

HR7511.reason: Laken Riley Act (118th) - same concept as HR29, requires ICE detention and increases immigration enforcement, opposing DACA-style protections

HR3009.reason: Enforce the Law for Sanctuary Cities Act - cuts federal funding to sanctuary jurisdictions that limit cooperation with federal immigration enforcement, opposing protections for undocumented immigrants

HR4038.reason: American Security Against Foreign Enemies Act (SAFE Act) - pauses Syrian and Iraqi refugee admissions and adds screening requirements, restricting immigration pathways

HR3486.reason: Stop Illegal Entry Act (119th) - criminalizes illegal entry more severely, increasing immigration enforcement that could affect DACA-eligible individuals
